sources.author: Dioscorides
sources.translation_reference: Beck, L.Y. (2005). Dioscorides: De Materia Medica. Hildesheim: Georg Olms Verlag.
sources.relevance: Primary source for Greco-Roman pharmacological knowledge. Documents medicinal uses of foods still recommended today.

sources.author: Hippocrates
sources.translation_reference: Loeb Classical Library. Hippocrates, Vol. IV.
sources.relevance: Father of Western medicine. Established diet and exercise as primary health interventions.

sources.author: Avicenna (Ibn Sina)
sources.translation_reference: Gruner, O.C. (1930). Avicenna: A Treatise on the Canon of Medicine. London: Luzac.
sources.relevance: Encyclopedic medical text used as standard in Europe and Islamic world for 600 years.

sources.author: Unknown (attributed to Shennong)
sources.work: Shennong Bencao Jing (Divine Farmer's Materia Medica)
sources.translation_reference: Bensky, D., Clavey, S., & Stöger, E. (2004). Chinese Herbal Medicine: Materia Medica.
sources.relevance: Oldest known Chinese pharmacopeia. Classifies 365 substances by toxicity and therapeutic value.

sources.author: Unknown (attributed to Charaka)
sources.translation_reference: Sharma, P.V. (1981). Charaka Samhita. Varanasi: Chaukhambha Orientalia.
sources.relevance: Foundational Ayurvedic text. Emphasizes digestion, diet, and mental health as pillars of longevity.

sources.author: Sushruta
sources.relevance: Foundational surgical and medical text. Includes early water purification and parasite treatment protocols.

sources.title: Primary Prevention of Cardiovascular Disease with a Mediterranean Diet
sources.key_finding: 30% reduction in major cardiovascular events with Mediterranean diet supplemented with extra-virgin olive oil.

sources.title: Association of Nut Consumption with Total and Cause-Specific Mortality
sources.key_finding: Nut consumption ≥7×/week associated with 20% lower all-cause mortality.

sources.title: Meditation Programs for Psychological Stress and Well-being: Systematic Review and Meta-analysis
sources.key_finding: Meditation improves anxiety, depression, and pain with effect sizes comparable to antidepressants (0.3–0.4).

sources.title: Daily Steps and All-Cause Mortality: A Meta-analysis of 15 Prospective Cohort Studies
sources.key_finding: Each 1,000-step increment associated with 6–36% lower mortality; benefits plateau ~7,000–10,000 steps.

sources.title: Reduced melanoma after regular sunscreen use: randomized trial follow-up
sources.key_finding: Daily sunscreen use reduces squamous cell carcinoma by 40% and melanoma by 50%.

sources.title: Gut-microbiota-targeted diets modulate human immune status
sources.key_finding: 10-week fermented food intervention increased microbiome diversity and decreased 19 inflammatory proteins including IL-6.

sources.title: Effects of Intermittent Fasting on Health, Aging, and Disease
sources.key_finding: Intermittent fasting improves glucose regulation, stress resistance, and reduces inflammation.

sources.title: Perspective: Casting light on sleep deficiency
sources.key_finding: Evening light exposure suppresses melatonin by >50% and shifts circadian clock by 3 hours.

sources.title: Whole grain consumption and risk of cardiovascular disease, cancer, and all cause mortality
sources.key_finding: Each 90 g/day increase in whole grains associated with 17% lower all-cause mortality.

sources.title: Prognostic value of grip strength: findings from the PURE study
sources.key_finding: Grip strength is a stronger predictor of all-cause mortality than systolic blood pressure.

sources.title: Evening use of light-emitting eReaders negatively affects sleep
sources.key_finding: Evening screen use reduces melatonin, impairs next-morning alertness, disrupts REM sleep.

sources.title: Developmental neurotoxicity of industrial chemicals
sources.key_finding: Chemical contaminants cause developmental neurotoxicity in millions of children worldwide.

sources.title: Can meditation slow rate of cellular aging?
sources.key_finding: Chronic stress associated with shorter telomeres; meditation increases telomerase activity.

sources.title: High dietary anthocyanin intake is associated with slower cognitive decline
sources.key_finding: ≥2 servings/week of blueberries/strawberries associated with up to 2.5 years delay in cognitive aging.

sources.title: Shift work and circadian disruption
sources.key_finding: Shift work involving circadian disruption classified as probably carcinogenic to humans (Group 2A).

sources.title: Sleep drives metabolite clearance from the adult brain
sources.key_finding: Sleep activates the glymphatic system — brain's waste clearance pathway removing amyloid-beta and tau.

grading_system.4: At least one RCT + strong mechanistic evidence
grading_system.3: Small human studies + consistent observational data
